commit.message:
Fix view toggle missing from mobile overflow menu on iOS

The card/list view toggle was not rendering in the SecondaryActions
dropdown on MAUI Blazor Hybrid (iOS WebView). Root cause: inline
ternary expressions in Razor markup (@onclick and @() class/text
bindings) caused the MAUI WebView Blazor renderer to silently drop
the entire <li> element from the render tree.

Fix: extract the ternary logic into computed properties
(ViewToggleIcon, ViewToggleLabel) and a ToggleViewMode method,
keeping the markup simple enough for the WebView renderer.

Verified on iOS 26.2 simulator via DevFlow CDP inspection —
the menu now shows all items including 'List View' toggle.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix filtered history re-root conflicts

Move the filtered tree into a temporary holding directory before renesting it under the target path. This avoids collisions when both repo-root files and nested Razor files share names like Directory.Build.props.

commit.message:
Implement UMDF feed synchronization state machine

State machine: WaitInstrumentDefinition → WaitSnapshot → CatchUp → RealTime
- Buffer incremental packets while consuming InstrDef and Snapshot cycles
- Track SecurityDefinition count via TotNoRelatedSym for cycle completion
- Detect snapshot cycle boundaries via SeqNum=1 wrap-around
- Track LastMsgSeqNumProcessed from SnapshotFullRefresh_Header_30
- On catch-up: discard queued incrementals with SeqNum <= snapshot, apply rest
- On gap in RealTime: transition to Recovery → re-consume snapshot
- ChannelHandler.HandlePacket now returns GapResult for caller decisions
- Console app: Ctrl+C support, WaitForCompletionAsync, order logging

fix(#113): fix long-press copy inconsistency on assistant messages (#116)

* fix(#113): fix long-press copy inconsistency on assistant messages

Replace combinedClickable on Surface with pointerInput(detectTapGestures)
on the outer Box container. combinedClickable uses PointerEventPass.Main
(bottom-up), so ClickableText composables inside MarkdownContent (which
also use Main pass) intercepted the gesture first and prevented the
long-press from being detected reliably on tall/rich assistant messages.

detectTapGestures calls awaitFirstDown(requireUnconsumed = false), so
the outer Box receives the down event regardless of child consumption.
Long-press is detected via a delay timeout — independent of whether
inner ClickableText nodes later consume pointer events.

User messages (plain Text, no ClickableText) were unaffected; this fix
makes assistant message long-press parity consistent across all content.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use CJS output format for non-ESM built-in extensions (#62)

The transpileExtensions() function in build/next/index.ts was hardcoding
'esm' format for all built-in extensions. This caused CJS extensions
(emmet, npm, merge-conflict, etc.) to output ESM import statements,
which then failed at runtime with 'Cannot use import statement outside
a module' when loaded via require().

Now each extension's package.json is read to determine the correct
output format: extensions with "type": "module" (only github) use ESM,
all others use CJS. This matches the gulp ESBuildTranspiler behavior.

Fixes #57

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(benchmarks): add conv_cifar_v3 suite for phase-3 GPU sweep

15 configurations in 7 groups targeting beyond 75% CIFAR-10 accuracy:

1. Random crop augmentation (pad-4 reflect + random crop), the standard
   CIFAR-10 augment used by CNN baselines, as single-model baseline.
2. n_augment sweep (2, 3 copies per batch) — accumulates more
   augmented variants into the normal-equation system.
3. 20k neurons + crop+flip augmentation (single model).
4. Ensemble ×5 + crop+flip with n_augment sweep.
5. Large ensembles (×10, ×20) with hflip and crop+flip.
6. Diverse patch-size ensembles (p3/p5/p7) ×6, ×9 with crop+flip.
7. Combined best: 20k neurons × 5 ensemble + crop+flip.

commit.message:
fix: graceful fallback when GitHub rejects inline review comments (#50)

## Problem

PR #49 review failed with 422 because the CLI generated inline comments
with line numbers not in the PR diff. The entire review was lost.

## Fix

When `post_review()` gets a 422 and has inline comments, it retries
without them — appending the comment text to the review body instead.
The review still gets posted, feedback isn't lost.

## Context

This is a natural consequence of the token refactor (#40) — the CLI
writes review JSON and the orchestrator posts it. The CLI doesn't know
which lines are in the diff, so it may guess wrong. This fallback
ensures the review always gets posted.

commit.message:
fix: register SIGTERM handler before initial sync cycle

Move signal handler registration to the top of main() so that
SIGTERM sent by Docker during the initial startup sync (which can
run for hours across 300+ TLDs) is correctly intercepted.

Previously the handler was only registered after the blocking
scheduler was created, meaning containers receiving SIGTERM during
the startup cycle would ignore it and keep running until SIGKILL
(30-minute grace period). This caused 3 simultaneous worker
containers during rolling deploys.

before common delimiters (in/into/then/etc), preventing trailing prose
from being captured as part of the formula.

BUG-2: Add dedicated target-cell extractor for 'in cell X' / 'into cell X'
patterns that runs before range extraction, so the target cell is preserved
even when a range appears inside the formula (e.g. =SUM(D2:D9)).

BUG-3: Pre-strip section header lines and include 'Section N:' in the
split regex so headers don't merge with preceding step text.

BUG-4: Suppress spurious CELL_VALUE tasks when a formula-type task
(FORMULA, LOOKUP_FUNCTION, etc.) already exists for the same line.

expected value or formula, instead of a confident pass.

commit.message:
feat: add error accumulation to Table and MutableTable

Replace silent no-ops with error accumulation across both APIs:

Table (immutable):
- Methods record errors via withErrf() instead of silently returning t
- Errors propagate through chains via newTableFrom()
- New methods: Errs(), HasErrs()

MutableTable (mutable):
- All mutation methods now return *MutableTable for chaining
- Methods that returned error (Map, Rename, Set, etc.) now accumulate
  errors via addErrf() instead
- Methods that silently returned on missing columns now record errors
- New methods: Errs(), HasErrs(), ResetErrs()
- Mutable()/Freeze() propagate errors between types

Usage:
  // Immutable chaining with error check
  result := t.Sort("missing", true).Map("col", fn)
  if result.HasErrs() { log.Println(result.Errs()) }

  // Mutable chaining with error check
  m.Sort("name", true).FillForward("region").Map("age", fn)
  if m.HasErrs() { log.Println(m.Errs()) }

* fix: use watch to trigger dashboard fetch when auth resolves in PortfolioView

Root cause: PortfolioView.onMounted fires before App.vue.onMounted's checkAuth()
completes (lazy-loaded components mount before their parent's onMounted). The
auth-gated fetchDashboard() was never called when authentication completed
asynchronously (after the component was already mounted).

Fix: Replace onMounted-only check with a watch({ immediate: true }) on
auth.isAuthenticated. This handles both:
  1. Auth already set when component mounts (loginAs flow)
  2. Auth set asynchronously after mount (checkAuth from App.vue onMounted)

The loading guard prevents double-fetches.
